input.plan:
1. Update `modules/locale.py` in the existing keyboard-layout switching section, because that file already owns Russian-layout command-mode and normal-mode bindings that wrap actions with `en(...)`.
2. Add a new binding for `пп` that uses the existing `en(cmd)` helper from `modules/base.py` so the action sequence is `switch-to-english` followed by the command equivalent of `gg`.
3. Verify the exact qutebrowser command form for the `gg` behavior from existing config conventions, keep the change minimal to one file, and then run lightweight validation on the edited file.

## 1. TASK
Add normal-mode binding: `пп` triggers `gg` behavior and switches to English layout

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME
- [ ] Files created/modified: `modules/locale.py`
- [ ] Functionality: In normal mode, typing `пп` scrolls to page top (equivalent of `gg`) AND switches keyboard layout to English (via `spawn -u switch-to-english`)
- [ ] Verification: `python -m py_compile modules/locale.py` passes (or repo-equivalent syntax check if present)

## 3. REQUIRED TOOLS
- read: inspect existing layout switching helpers in `modules/base.py` and current bindings in `modules/locale.py`
- grep: confirm no existing `пп` binding and see how other RU-layout bindings are implemented
- apply_patch: implement minimal change in `modules/locale.py`
- lsp_diagnostics: run on `modules/locale.py` after edit (if available)

## 4. MUST DO
- Use existing helper `en(cmd)` from `modules/base.py` (do not reimplement switching)
- Place the new bind next to other layout-switching binds in `modules/locale.py` (the “Auto-switch to English for command modes” section)
- Implement `gg` behavior via qutebrowser command `scroll-to-perc 0` wrapped in `en(...)` (preferred over faking keys)
- Keep binding in normal mode (default) unless you find an explicit mode is required by existing style
- Append a short note to `.sisyphus/notepads/pp-gg/learnings.md` about the exact command chosen for `gg`

## 5. MUST NOT DO
- Do NOT modify any other files besides `modules/locale.py`
- Do NOT add new dependencies or new helper functions
- Do NOT change unrelated keybindings
- Do NOT remove existing comments or reorder large sections

## 6. CONTEXT
### Notepad Paths
- READ: `.sisyphus/notepads/pp-gg/learnings.md`
- READ: `.sisyphus/notepads/pp-gg/issues.md`
- WRITE: Append to `.sisyphus/notepads/pp-gg/learnings.md`

### Inherited Wisdom
- `en(cmd)` already expands to `spawn -u switch-to-english ;; {cmd}` (see `modules/base.py`)
- Layout-aware bindings are centralized in `modules/locale.py`

### Dependencies
- None; this should be a single-line config.bind addition.

- Use existing helper `en(cmd)` (already imported in `qutebrowser/modules/locale.py`)
- Implement `G` behavior using qutebrowser command `scroll-to-perc 100` wrapped in `en(...)`
- Place the new bind adjacent to `config.bind("пп", en("scroll-to-perc 0"))` for consistency
- Keep change minimal: only add the binding line; do not reformat the file

## 5. MUST NOT DO
- Do NOT modify any files other than `qutebrowser/modules/locale.py`
- Do NOT touch `opencode/opencode.jsonc`
- Do NOT add new helpers, dependencies, or comments

- `en(cmd)` expands to `spawn -u switch-to-english ;; {cmd}` (`modules/base.py`)
- `пп` already maps to `en("scroll-to-perc 0")` in `qutebrowser/modules/locale.py`

### Notes
- Use uppercase Cyrillic `П` exactly as the binding string.

input.prompt: [CONTEXT] We are evaluating how to reorganize qutebrowser keybindings in this repo. Current files include modules/bindings.py and modules/locale.py. There are English bindings and Russian-layout duplicates, including leader (`space+...`) bindings and normal-mode bindings. [GOAL] Determine the current split of responsibilities and identify the cleanest refactor points for duplicating all relevant bindings while hiding Russian duplicates from keyhint. [DOWNSTREAM] I will use this to recommend a maintainable file/module organization before implementation. [REQUEST] Read the relevant binding files and summarize: 1) which files own which classes of bindings today, 2) where Russian duplicates already exist, 3) which patterns are repeated enough to factor, 4) any risks of splitting into separate EN/RU files. Skip implementation.

We need advice on organizing qutebrowser config bindings. Current repo has `modules/bindings.py` for most bindings and `modules/locale.py` for keyboard-layout-aware bindings using helper `en(cmd)` to switch to English before running a command. The user wants all normal-mode bindings and all leader (`space+...`) bindings duplicated for Russian keyboard layout, but hidden from qutebrowser's keyhint menu via `keyhint.blacklist`. They suggested splitting English and Russian bindings into two near-identical files. Please evaluate better organization options and recommend the most maintainable design. Address specifically: 1) separate EN/RU files vs generating RU duplicates from EN definitions, 2) how to keep leader duplicates hidden via blacklist, 3) how to minimize drift when new bindings are added, 4) what a clean module structure would look like in a Python qutebrowser config. Read-only advice only; do not modify files.

options.label: Generator specs (Recommended)
options.description: Один список биндов + автогенерация RU и keyhint blacklist

options.label: Two EN/RU files
options.description: Разнести бинды по двум файлам и поддерживать оба вручную

options.label: Discuss more first
options.description: Сначала уточнить структуру и границы миграции

input.summary: Refactor qutebrowser bindings to use one canonical binding spec list, generate Russian duplicates at config load time, and automatically hide Russian leader duplicates from keyhint.
input.plan:
1. Add a new helper module, likely `modules/keymap.py`, which will contain the EN→RU layout map and a `translate_keyseq(...)` function. This translator will handle plain sequences like `yy`, `ge`, `tm`, and modifier forms like `<Cmd-k>` and `<Ctrl-a>`, while leaving named keys like `<Escape>` untouched.

2. In the same helper layer, add binding application helpers such as `apply_binding(...)` / `apply_bindings(...)`. These helpers will:
- bind the canonical EN key,
- derive the RU equivalent,
- bind the RU key when it differs,
- optionally wrap the RU command in existing `en(...)`,
- collect RU leader bindings into a blacklist structure for `c.keyhint.blacklist`.

3. Add a data-only module, likely `modules/bindings_defs.py`, containing the canonical binding specs. Each spec will describe one binding once, with fields like:
- `key`
- `command`
- `mode` (default normal)
- `duplicate_ru` (default true)
- `wrap_ru_with_en` (default true for normal/leader bindings)
- `hide_ru_in_keyhint` (true for leader bindings, false for plain normal bindings unless explicitly needed)
This becomes the single source of truth for normal-mode and `leader` bindings.

4. Refactor `modules/bindings.py` so it no longer manually duplicates EN/RU bindings. Instead, it will import the specs and helper functions, apply all bindings, and then merge the generated blacklist into `c.keyhint.blacklist`.

5. Keep `modules/locale.py` focused on layout behavior and true special cases: insert-mode enter/leave behavior, prompt-opening bindings, and any bindings that intentionally preserve layout instead of switching to EN. If some existing bindings there are just ordinary bind definitions, migrate them into specs where possible.

6. During the migration, preserve all current behavior:
- all existing English normal-mode bindings remain,
- all current Russian duplicates remain functional,
- all `space+...` Russian duplicates are hidden from keyhint,
- plain normal-mode Russian duplicates stay visible or hidden according to the chosen spec flags,
- special cases already using `en(...)` keep doing so.

7. Add a lightweight safety check during application: if two different EN specs translate to the same RU keychain in the same mode, raise a clear error or warning. This prevents silent collisions during future additions.

8. Verification after implementation:
- run `python -m py_compile` on changed modules,
- run `lsp_diagnostics` on all changed Python files,
- start qutebrowser with this config to ensure the config loads,
- verify that EN and RU bindings both work,
- verify that RU `leader` duplicates no longer appear in the keyhint menu after pressing `Space`.

9. Migration strategy: do this in one refactor pass rather than piecemeal. The helper/generator should land first, then existing manual duplicates can be removed only after the generated equivalents are in place and verified.

## 1. TASK
- [ ] Add `modules/keymap.py` with EN->RU keychain translation + binding application helper

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME
- [ ] Files created/modified: `modules/keymap.py`
- [ ] Functionality: provide helper(s) to (a) translate qutebrowser keychains from EN layout to RU layout, and (b) apply a binding spec by binding EN key + RU key (if different) and returning data needed to hide RU keys from keyhint
- [ ] Verification: `python -m py_compile modules/keymap.py` passes

## 3. REQUIRED TOOLS
- read: `modules/base.py`, `modules/bindings.py`, `modules/locale.py` (to match existing style and `en()`/`leader` helpers)
- grep: find existing RU duplicates patterns and `<Cmd-...>` usage
- apply_patch: create `modules/keymap.py`

## 4. MUST DO
- Implement an EN->RU mapping covering ASCII letters a-z and A-Z on QWERTY->Russian layout (e.g. a->ф, b->и, ...); include uppercase mapping.
- Implement `translate_keychain(key: str) -> str`:
  - translate raw sequences like `yy`, `ge`, `tm` character-by-character.
  - translate modifier tokens like `<Cmd-k>` -> `<Cmd-л>` and `<Ctrl-a>` -> `<Ctrl-ф>`.
  - DO NOT translate named keys inside angle brackets (e.g. `<Escape>`, `<Left>`, `<Shift>`, `<Cmd-Left>`).
  - Preserve anything already non-ASCII (Cyrillic) unchanged.
- Implement a small helper like `apply_binding(config, c, *, key, command, mode='normal', duplicate_ru=True, wrap_ru_with_en=True, hide_ru_in_keyhint=False, en_wrapper=en) -> dict`:
  - always binds EN `key`.
  - if `duplicate_ru`, binds RU key when different.
  - if RU key bound and `wrap_ru_with_en`, wrap the command with provided `en_wrapper`.
  - if RU key bound and `hide_ru_in_keyhint`, collect that RU key for blacklist.
  - detect collisions by keeping a per-mode set/dict of bound RU keys (within this apply call scope; you can expose a state object or allow caller to pass in a registry).
- Keep API minimal and practical for upcoming refactor.
- Append any gotchas/decisions to `.sisyphus/notepads/bindings-generator/learnings.md` (append only).

## 5. MUST NOT DO
- Do NOT modify any files except `modules/keymap.py` and `.sisyphus/notepads/bindings-generator/learnings.md`
- Do NOT touch `opencode/opencode.jsonc` or other unrelated files
- Do NOT add external dependencies

## 6. CONTEXT
### Notepad Paths
- READ: `.sisyphus/notepads/bindings-generator/learnings.md`
- READ: `.sisyphus/notepads/bindings-generator/issues.md`
- WRITE: Append to `.sisyphus/notepads/bindings-generator/learnings.md`

### Inherited Wisdom
- We want one canonical EN binding spec list and auto-generated RU duplicates.
- RU leader duplicates should be hidden using `c.keyhint.blacklist` derived from generated RU keys.
- Existing `en(cmd)` helper in `modules/base.py` expands to `spawn -u switch-to-english ;; {cmd}`.

### Dependencies
- Next tasks will create `modules/bindings_defs.py` and refactor `modules/bindings.py` to use these helpers.

- [ ] Functionality: `modules/bindings.py` now imports `BINDING_SPECS` from `modules/bindings_defs.py` and `apply_binding` from `modules/keymap.py`. It iterates over the specs and applies them. It also collects all generated RU blacklist entries and merges them into `c.keyhint.blacklist`.
- [ ] Verification: `python -m py_compile modules/bindings.py` passes.

## 3. REQUIRED TOOLS
- read: `modules/bindings.py`, `modules/bindings_defs.py`, `modules/keymap.py`
- edit: refactor `modules/bindings.py`

## 4. MUST DO
- Clean up `modules/bindings.py` by removing all manual `config.bind` calls that are now covered by `BINDING_SPECS`.
- Import `BINDING_SPECS` from `modules/bindings_defs.py`.
- Import `apply_binding` from `modules/keymap.py`.
- Create a loop to apply all specs:
  ```python
  from modules.bindings_defs import BINDING_SPECS
  from modules.keymap import apply_binding
  from collections import defaultdict

  ru_registry = {} # Or as required by your apply_binding implementation
  full_blacklist = defaultdict(list)

  for spec in BINDING_SPECS:
      res = apply_binding(
          config, c,
          key=spec.key,
          command=spec.command,
          mode=spec.mode,
          duplicate_ru=spec.duplicate_ru,
          wrap_ru_with_en=spec.wrap_ru_with_en,
          hide_ru_in_keyhint=spec.hide_ru_in_keyhint,
          ru_registry=ru_registry
      )
      for mode, keys in res["keyhint_blacklist"].items():
          full_blacklist[mode].extend(keys)
  ```
- Merge `full_blacklist` into `c.keyhint.blacklist`:
  ```python
  for mode, keys in full_blacklist.items():
      # Ensure c.keyhint.blacklist exists for this mode
      # Assuming it's a list or similar depending on qutebrowser version
      # Check documentation or existing config for correct merge logic
      # Usually c.keyhint.blacklist is a list of strings
      c.keyhint.blacklist.extend(keys)
  ```
- Keep any special logic in `modules/bindings.py` that was NOT moved to specs (e.g. unbinds, complex variables, or bindings that don't fit the spec pattern).
- **CRITICAL**: Do NOT touch any other files in the repo. The user is working on them in other sessions.
- Append findings to `.sisyphus/notepads/bindings-generator/learnings.md`.

## 1. TASK
- [ ] Update `modules/locale.py` to keep only layout-behavior and migrate eligible bindings to specs

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME
- [ ] Files modified: `modules/locale.py`, `modules/bindings_defs.py`
- [ ] Functionality:
  - All standard leader and normal-mode bindings that were in `modules/locale.py` (like `leader + ss`, `leader + sw`, `ge`, `пп`, `П`, etc.) are moved to `BINDING_SPECS` in `modules/bindings_defs.py`.
  - `modules/locale.py` is pruned to contain ONLY insert-mode behavior overrides (`en_mode_enter`, `en_mode_leave`, `config.bind("i", ...)` etc.) and prompt-opening bindings that are truly layout-behavioral (like `:` and `t` bindings).
  - RU versions for moved bindings are removed from `locale.py` (they'll be auto-generated).
- [ ] Verification: `python -m py_compile modules/locale.py modules/bindings_defs.py` passes.

## 3. REQUIRED TOOLS
- read: `modules/locale.py`, `modules/bindings_defs.py`
- edit: move bindings from `locale.py` to `bindings_defs.py`.

## 4. MUST DO
- Move these from `locale.py` to `bindings_defs.py`: `пп`, `П`, `leader + leader`, `ge`, `leader + ss`, `leader + sw`, `leader + sl`, `leader + sL`, `leader + sd`, `leader + sr`, `leader + tm`, `leader + tw`, `leader + cS`.
- For moved bindings that use `en(...)` on the EN side, set `wrap_ru_with_en=False` in the spec to avoid double wrapping.
- For moved leader bindings, ensure `hide_ru_in_keyhint=True`.
- Remove the Russian-only bindings (like `config.bind("е", ...)`) from `locale.py` since they will now be auto-generated from the EN specs.
- Keep in `locale.py`: insert mode auto-behavior settings, `en_mode_enter`, `en_mode_leave`, and the bindings for `i`, `ш`, `a`, `ф`, `leader + aa`, `<Escape>`, `:`, `t`. (Note: `:` and `t` could also move to specs, but they are very layout-sensitive, so keeping them in locale is fine for now).

## 5. MUST NOT DO
- Do NOT modify `modules/bindings.py` or `modules/keymap.py`.
- Do NOT touch `opencode/opencode.jsonc`.

## 6. CONTEXT
- You are completing the reorganization by ensuring all "standard" bindings live in the spec list, and `locale.py` stays focused on the mechanics of layout switching.

Shows diffstat after locale.py and bindings_defs.py updates
